title: PESTO campaign scheduling UI: banner toggle vs. dropdown
summary: Does a scheduling dropdown instead of a banner toggle increase PESTO adoption? Yes, it did.
hypothesis: If we move PESTO into the scheduling flow as a dropdown option instead of a banner toggle, users will be more likely to adopt it because it fits naturally into the scheduling workflow.
results: The dropdown treatment increased PESTO adoption by 19% (p < 0.01). Users in the treatment group were significantly more likely to enable PESTO scheduling.
learning: Placing PESTO as a contextual option within the scheduling flow—rather than as a prominent banner—reduces friction and increases adoption by meeting users where they already are in the workflow.
notes: Design screenshots in Slack thread. Figma link includes treatment designs only.
